def: adj. Deficient, less, short; little, small, inconsiderable; abated; scanty; — adv. Rarely, seldom (the term is much used as the first member of compounds, and generally imports the idea of negation, defectiveness, or inferiority)

Afflicted; accursed; wretched. kam-pā , Opp. of der pā , adj. 1. Not lasting or durable; frail. 2. Transient. kam paidārārī , n. f. A deficient harvest. kam-tar , adj. Less; lesser; smaller.

def: v.t. To earn, to acquire, get; to accumulate, save; to work, labour; to perform; to do (good); to perpetrate, commit (a crime); to exercise (the faculties) to clean or curry (leather); to clean (a privy, &c.); to dress or prepare (land)
more: v. a. S. कर्म 1. To earn; get. 1. Dharam kamānā . 2. Pāp kamānā . 3. Unhoṅ ne baṛā rupayā kamāyā . 4. Rupae ko rupayā kamātā hai . Prov. Money makes money. 5. Kamāveṅ Khān-khānāṅ. uṛāveṅ mīyāṅ fahīm . Prov. The master earns, the canny servant spends. 6. Kamāne khāne kī bāteṅ haiṅ . By such arts a living is made. 2. To work; labor; drudge. 3. To exercise (faculties); train; develope. 4. To clean or curry (leather). Chamṛā kamānā . 5. To clean a chamber pot. Mehtrānī gubār dil se jhāṛ! Kam kam-ānā terā qayāmat hai. Zilā . A pun. 6. To prepare the land; to enrich; fertilize. Zamīn kamānā . 7. To live by prostitution. Kharchī kamānā. kamā khānā , v. a. To live by one's own earnings. kamā lenā , v. n. To wear away; consume. Bīmārī ne kamā līyā .
v. a. 1. To earn. 2. To work, to perform, to perpetrate, to commit (crime), to do (good). 3. To clean (leather or a privy), to curry. 4. (from Pers. کم) To lessen, to abate.

ety: Pehl. kamar; Zend kamara, fr. kamar = S. kmar
def: s.f. The loins, the waist; a girdle, zone, belt; the middle (of a mountain, &c.); the flank (of an army); an arch
more: n. f. 1. The loins; waist. 1. Māṅgne par kamar bāṅdh lī hai, mahnat nahīṅ ho saktī (Said to a beggar). You have girt your loins to beg, not to work. 2. Kālā hai, par kāg nahīṅ pātāl rahe, par nāg nahīṅ; Patlī kamar hai, chītā nahīṅ; uchak chaṛhe, laṅgūr nahīṅ . Riddle. Ans. The large black ant. It's black, a crow it's not; lives under ground, a snake it's not; It's waist is thin, a leopard not; it bounds, an ape it's not. 2. The middle. 3. A belt. kamar bāṅdhnā, yā kasnā , v. a. 1. To gird up one's loins; to arm. 2. To prepare. kamar-bastā , adj. Having the loins girt; armed; prepared. kamar-band , n. m. 1. A girdle; zone; belt; waistband. 2. A long piece of cloth girt round the loins. kamar-bandī , n. f. 1. Arming; preparation for battle, etc. 2. Military uniform. kamar pakaṛ-ke uṭhnā , v. n. To become weak or feeble. kamar pakaṛnā yā thāmnā , v. a. 1. To grip one by the waist. 2. To su…

s. f. 1. The loins, the waist. 2. A girdle, a belt, zone. 3. An arch. 4. The flank (of an army). 5. The middle (of a mountain). کمرباندهنا kamar bāndhnā, To begird; to get ready, to resolve, to be intent, to arm. کمربسته kamar-basta, adj. Having the loins girt; ready. کمربند kamar-band, 1. s. m. A girdle, a zone, waist-band. 2. adj. Alert, ready for battle, prepared, armed. کمربندي kamar-band-ī, s. f. Arming, preparation for battle, &c. کمربندي کرنا kamar-bandī karnā, To arm. کمربيٿهني kamar-baiṭhnī (dakh.) To become weak or dejected. کمرپکڙکي اٿهنا kamar pakaṛke uṭhnā, To be weak. کمرپکڙنا kamar pakaṛnā, To urge a claim against one. کمرٿوٿنا kamar ṭūṭnā, 1. To be hopeless. 2. To be deserted by oneʼs friends. کمرتوڙنا kamar toṛnā, 1. To take away oneʼs hopes. 2. To draw over oneʼs friends or adherents to the side of oneʼs enemies. کمر دوال kamar-doʼāl, A leathern belt. کمرره جانا kamar r…
subs: (-par), To gird up the loins (for), to get ready, prepare, arm (for); to resolve, to be intent (on)
adj. Having the loins girt, in a state of readiness, on the alert, ready for action, prepared, armed
adj. = kamar-basta; — s.m. A girdle, zone, sash, belt, waistband; a long piece of cloth folded round the loins; the string with which drawers, &c. are fastened (syn. izār-band)
(-kī), To encourage, inspirit, give hope of success (to), to stimulate (one to persevere)
s.f. Girding up the loins, preparing, getting ready; preparation (for action), prepared state, alertness
s.m. A zone, or cincture (of gold or silver); — a tie-beam
'To rise holding the loins'; to be in a weak or feeble state
or kamar thāmnā (-kī), To seize (one) by the waist; to urge a claim (against one); — to support, back, help, encourage
adj. (f. -ī), Gone in the loins; impotent; — hump-backed
(-kī), 'The loins or back to be broken'; to be deserted by (one's) friends; to give way or sink under the loss of a child, &c.; to be deprived of hope, to lose confidence or courage
(-kī), To break the back (of); to take away all hope (from), to discourage, &c.; to alienate one's friends or adherents
The loins or back to give way; to have a pain in the loins (from long standing); the back to become palsied
To stretch one self out for brief repose, to lie down
or kamar kasnā = kamar bāṅdhnā, q.v.

s.f. Ungirding the loins; undoing or opening the waistband; (Mil.) the order to take off accoutrements; — a fee levied by a government peon from a person over whom he is placed in charge for permission to perform the common functions of life
s.m. The part of a beam which projects outside a wall
To undo or throw off (one's) belt, &c.; to quit service; to take (one's) ease
(-ko), To have a sore back (a horse); to get a back-ache (through lying too long in bed)
(-kī), To strike sideways; to attack (an army) in flank
To take courage or heart; to undertake a work of expense
'To shake or move the loins'; to endeavour; — to copulate.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کَمَر (kamar). First attested in c. 1635 as Middle Hindi کمر (kmr /⁠kamar⁠/). Displaced Middle Hindi لنک (lãk, “waist”).

def: s.m. A blanket ( = kammal); a garment of wool
more: n. m. kamblī, kāmlī , n. f. S. कम्बल Pālī, kambalo . A coarse blanket. 1. Thoṛe mol kī kāmlī kare baṛoṅ kā kām, Mahmūdī aur bāftā sab ke rākhe mān . Dohā. A blanket cheap great service does, respect to silks and lace it shows. (It protects them from being soiled). 2. Faqīr apnī kamli hī meṅ khush hai . Prov. The beggar is happy in his blanket. 3. Tulsī! kārī kamrī chaṛhe na dūjā raṅg . Tulsī! the blanket black no other hue will take. kambal-posh , n. m. A mendicant whose only garment is a blanket. apnī kamlī meṅ mast h. , v. n. To be indifferent ( apnī khāl meṅ mast honā ).
subs: adj. & s.m. Wearing a blanket; — a mendicant whose only gament is a blanket.

H. P., n. m. Manager; agent ( kārkun ). kām-dār , adj. Worked; embroidered.

s. m. The god of love, the Indian Cupid. He was the son of Vish̤ṇu and Rukmiṇī, and husband of Rati (or Venus). Having offended Mahādeva, he was destroyed by the flame from the eyes of the angry Deity. He was, however, born again as Pradyumna, son of Kr̤ish̤ṇa; but was immediately seized by Sambara (whom it had been foretold he would destroy), and thrown into the ocean; where he was swallowed by a fish, which being caught was presented to Sambara; and, the child, on being found in the belly of the fish, was committed to Rati, who reared him, till he accomplished the prophesy in destroying Sambara. See more under رتي ratī.
s.m. = S کامروپ कामरूप kām-rūp, adj. See s.v. kāma or kām.

A method of transmuting words, so as to form, in writing, a cypher, and in speech a gibberish or cant language, unintelligible to all who do not possess the key (the letters k and m, ṣ and lā, a and w, ḥ and t̤, l and h, d and r, s and ʻa, are mutually interchanged for one another; and all those which have diacritical points remain unchanged; the word salāmat written according to this rule becomes عصکت).
more: s. m. A method of transmuting words, so as to form, in writing, a cypher, and in speech, a gibberish or cant language, unintelligible to all who do not possess the key. The rule is contained in the following couplet: کم صلا اوحط له درسع حرف منقوطرا بجايش دع kam ṣalā au ḥatuṉ lahu dar saﻌ; ḥarf-i-man- ḳūt̤rā bajāyish daﻌ. That is, the letters composing the following pairs are mutually interchanged for one another, and all those which have diacritical points remain unaltered; ع س; رد;ه ل; ط ح; وا; لاص; م ک. The word سلامت written according to this rule becomes عصکت.

s.m. Canaan (the country).
wety: Borrowed from Arabic كَنْعَان (kanʕān).

def: s.m. A wood, grove, forest; desert; a house; — (ka+ān˚) the face of Brahmā.
more: s. m. A forest, desert, wood.

ety: kan, q.v.+anda = Zend añṭ = S. ant
def: part. adj. & s.m. Digging; — a digger; — an instrument for digging.
part. & s.m. Making; doing; — maker; doer; factor, agent.
adj. & s.m. Of or belonging to Canaan; — a Canaanite.
s.f. Corner of the eye; side-glance, &c. (see kankhī)
subs: (-ko), To look at through the corner of the eye, to take a side glance at: to cast sheep's eyes at.

def: E. n. f. S. कोण corner, अक्षि eye. 1. The corners of the eye. 2. Sidelong looks or glances; sheep's eyes. kan-aṅkhiyoṅ, yā kankhī se dekhnā , v. a. To cast sheep's eyes.

n. f. 1. Whispering; speaking in a low tone. Kānā-bātī, kānā-bātī kur ... r ... r ... r . (Blown into a child's ear). 2. Plot; intrigue. kānā phūsī, yā kānā-bātī k. , v. a. To whisper; speak in a low tone. Pūt se apṛeṅ mujhe piṭvāve, kar kar-ke kānā-bātī, Sāsar ne maut nahīṅ ātī! Rus. Wom. Her son to strike me she puts up by whispering in his ear, why dont you die, Oh mother-in-law! kānoṅ par hāth rakhnā , v. n. To deny; refuse. kānoṅ kān na jānnā , v. n. lit . not to know one ear from the other. To be totally ignorant of. Unke ghar kī bāt koī kānoṅ kān nahīṅ jāntā. kānoṅ kī ṭheṅṭhīyāṅ nikalvānā , v. a. 1. See kān dikhānā . 2. To be cured of deafness. kānoṅ meṅ uṅgliyāṅ denā , v. n. lit . To put one's finger in one's ears. To close one's ears. kānoṅ meṅ bol mārnā , v. n. To pretend not to hear. kānoṅ menṅ ras paṛnā , v. n. To enjoy sweet sounds or voices, music, etc. kānā kachchū kā sāg , n. m. A potherb.
s.m. Side, margin, edge; — adv. Aside; — kinār, s.f. Embracing, hugging; embrace, bosom
subs: (-se), To keep aloof (from); to give up, quit, leave
adv. Aside, apart, out of the question, a thing apart; let alone; not to speak of, not to mention

ety: Pehl. kanār; Zend karana, fr. kar, 'to cut' = S. कर् (कृणोति, कृणाति)
def: s.m. Side, edge, margin, bank, shore, coast, beach, strand; limit, boundary; end; border, hem; tag end (of a piece of cloth)
more: n. m. Z. karānā . See حاشيه (1). 1. Side; edge; margin; shore; coast. 2. ( Bot .) Margin ( margo ). 3. End; terminus. 4. The tag end of a piece of cloth. kinārā chaṛhānā , v. a. To make a border or hem. kinārā k., kinārā-kashī k., kinārā-kash h. , v. a. To retire; keep aloof ( bāz ānā ). kināra-kashī , n. f. Turning aside; withdrawal. kināre par , adv. 1. Ashore. 2. Aside. kināre par lagā huā , adj. Anchored (a ship); landed. kināre-dār , ( Bot .) Bordered. kināre rakhnā , v. n. To put aside. kināre kināre , adv. Along the shore; alongside. kināre kināre chalnā , v. a. 1. To walk along the bank of a river. 2. To walk apart. kināre lagānā , v. a. To bring or drag to shore (a boat); lay or hove to (a ship). Merī naiyā kinare lagā, khivaiyā! Song. kināre lagṅā , v. n. 1. To be put to shore. 2. To come to an end. kināre h . To turn to one side. donoṅ kināre , Both sides or ends. maraṅ kināre…

(or prop. kanāra ) s. m. Side, margin, shore, strand, bank (of a river, &c.), beach, border, coast, part, limit, boundary, edge. کنارا پکڙنا kinārā pakaṛnā, To retire. کنارا کرنا kinārā karnā, v. a. To abstain, to refrain, to keep aloof, retire or withdraw (from), to lay aside. کناره کش kināra-kash, Withdrawing. کناري kināre or کناري پر kināre par, Aside, ashore. کناري چڙهنا kināre chaṛhnā, v. n. To run ashore, to strand. کناري هوجانا kināre ho-jānā, To retire, to withdraw.
subs: (with loc.), To put a border or hem (to)
vulg. kināre-dār, adj. Having a border, bordered
(-se), To keep or hold (oneself) aloof (from), to avoid, shun; to abstain (from), refrain (from); to retire, withdraw; — to lay aside
adj. Drawing aside (from), holding aloof; withdrawing, retiring, &c.
s.f. The act of drawing or turning aside (from), avoiding; withdrawal, &c.; — kināra-kashī karnā (-se) = kināra karnā, q.v.

or kināre-par, postpn. & adv. By the side or margin (of, -ke), along-side (of); on the bank (of); — ashore, on land; aside, apart, out of the question; a thing apart; let alone; not to speak of or mention ( = dar-kinār)
v.n. To run ashore, to strand
(-ke), Along-side (of); along the bank or shore; — aside, apart
(-ko), To put to shore, to beach (a boat); to moor; to lay to (a ship)
v.n. To reach the shore; to put to shore; — to reach the end (of), come to an end
v.n. To move or step aside, to get out of the way; to turn aside (from); to withdraw, to retire ( = kināra karnā).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کَنَارَه (kanāra).

def: v. n. (dakh.) To groan, &c. see کراهنا karāhnā.
s.f. A cornice.
s.f. A disease or blight in young sugar-cane (caused by the worm called kaṅswā).
more: n. f. A disease in īkh , caused by the kaṅsvā , q. v.

def: s.m. = kunisht, q.v. s.m. A temple (of idolaters, or fire-worshippers); a Christian church; a Jewish synagogue.
more: (perhaps from Arab. کنيست which from Heb. Chald. To collect, to assemble) s. m. A christian church, a jewish synagogue, a temple of idolaters.

ety: prob. for A. قناة 'a cane'; 'the back-bone'
def: s.f. The wall of a tent; a canvas or do - sūtī enclosure round a tent, or with which a sort of courtyard is formed in camp; a screen of canvas or other coarse cloth
more: n. f. The wall of a tent, or an external screen of canvas surrounding a tent. qanāt ghernā, yā kheṅchnā , v. a. To make an enclosure round a tent.
s. f. The walls of a tent, or canvass enclosure with which a sort of court yard is formed in camp, a screen.
subs: or qanāt khainćnā (with loc.), To surround with a wall or screen of canvas; to make an enclosure round (a tent, &c.).

def: adj. Enclosed in, or surrounded by, a qanāt
subs: s.f. An open space enclosed with qanāts, and used as a place of worship.

def: adj. = kanauṅḍā, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f.-ī), Averting the face (from), shrinking from meeting, shunning (another); standing in awe (of); abashed for favours received; favoured, under an obligation; bashful, ashamed; diffident; — disgraced, branded; — one who shrinks from, or tries to avoid meeting, a person to whom he is under an obligation; a shy or bashful person, &c.
subs: (or kanauṅṛā or kanauṛā) karnā (-ko), To put (one) under an obligation; to cause (one) to feel shame; to gain ascendency over (one), to cause (one) to stand in awe of; — to stain, brand, disgrace
prov. 'A blind eye is always in the way of accidents; and one is sure to meet the person whom he wishes to avoid'; a sore, and a person whom one wishes to avoid, are always in the way.

def: s.m. The town and country of Canouj (Kanouj) in the North-west Provinces of Hindūstān (where Porus is supposed to have reigned. The name would appear to be sometimes synonymous with Antar-bed. Other forms of spelling are kannoj, kinnoj, nd kinnauj).
adj. & s.m. Of or belonging to Kanauj; — an inhabitant of Kanauj; — the designation of various tribes originally from the country of Kanauj.

ety: prob. S. कर्ण+आवर्तः, 'turning the side-face towards'
def: s.m. Shyness, bashfulness, diffidence, shame; desire to avoid or shun (esp. a person to whom one is under an obligation); — stain, stigma; flaw, defect.
n. f. 1. Diffidence; shame. 2. Stain; stigma. 3. Flaw; defect.

adj. 1. Diffident; ashamed. Ke sartā nāhīṅ chūsat paunḍā, māṅg pavanḍ sūṅ hoë kanauṅḍā? What! cannot you live without sucking a sugarcane that you must demean yourself by begging for it from the husbandman? 2. Disgraced; branded. 3. Under an obligation. kanauṅḍā k. , v. a. 1. To put to the blush; put to shame. 2. To stain; brand; disgrace. 3. To put one under an obligation.
more: Tir. n. m. S. क्री A purchaser ( /?/kharīdār ). Hamrā le tūṅ bar kinvaiyā! ham chukaulīṅau, tūṅ le lihalā? Mag. Wom. What a great buyer you are to be sure! I made the bargain and you snap it up.

def: adj. & s.m. (f.-ī), Averting the face (from), shrinking from meeting, shunning (another); standing in awe (of); abashed for favours received; favoured, under an obligation; bashful, ashamed; diffident; — disgraced, branded; — one who shrinks from, or tries to avoid meeting, a person to whom he is under an obligation; a shy or bashful person, &c.
more: adj. Diffident, bashful, favoured, standing in awe of, or afraid to meet another.
subs: (or kanauṅṛā or kanauṛā) karnā (-ko), To put (one) under an obligation; to cause (one) to feel shame; to gain ascendency over (one), to cause (one) to stand in awe of; — to stain, brand, disgrace
prov. 'A blind eye is always in the way of accidents; and one is sure to meet the person whom he wishes to avoid'; a sore, and a person whom one wishes to avoid, are always in the way.

def: n. m. A corner ( konā , 1.).
s.m. Any situation in an apartment close to an angle of the wall; angle or corner (of a room or house).
more: s. m. Any situation in an apartment close to an angle of the wall.

def: s.f. The ear of a horse; pricking up the ear (a horse); — an ear-ring; — side-hair, side-lock
more: n. f. 1. An earring. 2. Pricking up the ears (a horse). 3. Side hair. kanautiyāṅ badalnā yā khaṛī karnā , v. a. To prick up the ears (a horse). kan'var , n. m. A boil on the temple.
subs: or kanautiyāṅ khaṛī karnā, To prick up the ears (a horse).

ety: prob. S. कक्ष्यः; Prk. कच्छो? cf. kāṅkh
def: s.m. A disorder in which the intestinum rectum is prolapsed, a prolapsus ani
more: n. f. S. काच 1. Glass. 1. Samman! chūṛī kāṅch kī kauṛī kauṛī dekh, Jab gal lāgī pīv ke lākh lākh kī ek . Samman. Samman! these bangles made of glass one kauṛī each you see, Her arms twined round her lover's neck each worth a lakh will be. 2. Tulsī! apne Rām ko yūṅ bhajye jaise lūṭ, Yoh tan ghaṛā hai kāṅch kā, tan meṅ jāëgd phāṭ . Tulsi! give Rām your heart as one a booty takes, As frail as glass, a moment and this body breaks. 2. A coarse glass made from ūsar or reh . 3. Prolapsus ani. kāṅch nikālnā , v. a. To beat to a jelly. kāṅch nikalnā . Prolapse of the anus; Ani procidentia. kāṅch'ū . ( Slang ) n. m. A thorough sodomite. nīlā kāṅch . Stone blue.
1. adj. Raw. 2. s. m. A disorder, in which the intestinum rectum is prolapsed; a prolapsus ani. کانچ نکالنا kānch nikālnā, Ani procidentia.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi کانچ (kanc /⁠kā̃c⁠/) (c. 1699) / کاچ (kac /⁠kāc⁠/) (c. 1564), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀓𑀘𑁆𑀘 (kacca), from Sanskrit काच (kāca).

def: s.m. = S. kāća or kāć, q.v.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi کانچ (kanc /⁠kā̃c⁠/) (c. 1699) / کاچ (kac /⁠kāc⁠/) (c. 1564), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀓𑀘𑁆𑀘 (kacca), from Sanskrit काच (kāca).

def: adj. (prov.) = kaććā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), Undressed, uncooked, unboiled; unripe, raw, green; immature, crude, undigested; unfinished, imperfect, incomplete; rude, rough, sketchy; imperfectly known or acquired; imperfectly skilled or practised (person); uninstructed, inexperienced, unknowing, simple, silly; unsteady, unstable; timid, faint-hearted; of imperfect make or texture, slight, inferior; weak, frail, brittle; young, tender (understanding); — built of unbaked bricks, built of mud; — unsound, false; disturbed, broken (sleep); not fast (as colour); not permanent, temporary (an appointment); not come to a head, hard, blind (as a boil); deficient, defective, minor, smaller, less; below the standard (of weight or measure); — unauthenticated, uncertain, doubtful, vague, not to be relied on

s.m. Two-fifths of a pakkā bīghā, q.v.

adj. & s.m. Half-cooked or baked, partly dressed; half-ripe; built partly of baked bricks and partly of mud; containing both the original rough and the finally drawn out or wrought up; crude, doubtful, uncertain, not to be relied on, not established or verified; — brickwork with mud for mortar
v.t. To dress, cook, or fry partially; — to settle (a bargain)
s.m. Cotton loosely spun into thread; raw cotton, thread or yarn
s.m. A stupid person, a fool
s.m. Unvarnished story, bonâ fide account
adj. Soft-hearted, tender-hearted; timid; deficient in courage, unmanly
s.m. Paint or colouring matter unprepared by boiling; not fast colour, colour (of cloth) that will wash out
v.t. To make angry, to irritate; — to discourage; to restrain (one, from a design, &c.), to withhold or keep back; to shame, to abash

or kaććā honā, v.n. To become irritated or angry, to get into a rage; — to lose heart, to become discouraged; to restrain or withhold oneself, to forbear; to hold back (through fear, or other motive); — to be put to shame, to be abashed

A temporary cultivator, a cultivator who has no permanent or hereditary right of occupation; a farmer, or contractor, or tenant, or applicant, &c. disqualified by bankruptcy or deficient means; — a temporary post or appointment
s.m. pl. Rude or unpractised writing, an unformed hand
Clay bricks (not baked, but dried in the sun)
A dish made by boiling rice in soup, placing layers of rice and meat one on top of the other and allowing to simmer
or kaćće din, s.m. pl. The first four or five months of pregnancy
The primary hearing of a case
or kaććī āmdanī = kaććā upaj, q.v.
A road that is not metalled or macadamized
The raw or tumid itch ( = kaććā koṛh)
v.n. lit. 'To play with mud marbles'; to do anything raw, or inexperienced, or childish
lit. 'To make (one) bring water with a pitcher of unbaked clay'; to make one pass through a difficult ordeal.

def: s.m. Gold; wealth; — adj. (f. -ī), Golden, made or consisting of gold
subs: adj. (f. -ī), Golden, consisting of gold; abounding in gold.

def: s.m. Gold
more: n. m. S. काञ्चन, Pālī, kanchanam . 1. Gold ( sonā ). 2. The prostitute class. 3. A whoreson. kanchan-bachchah , n. m. The son of a dancing girl. kanchan-chūr , n. m. A superior kind of rice. Usually kept for rare occasions, as weddings, entertainments, etc. It is so called because it turns yellow and improves by being kept two or three years. kanchan-nīr , n. m. lit . gold water. Clear water.
s. m. A cast of people whose females are dancers. کنچنبان kanchan-bān, A real kanchan, or one by birth.
subs: s.m. A superior kind of rice (so called because it turns yellow and improves by being kept two or three years)

def: s.m. A caste of people (somewhat akin to our gipsies) whose females are generally dancers, &c.
subs: s.m. A real or genuine kaṅćan by birth
s.m. The son or a kaṅćan, a whoreson.

def: s.f. = kanćinī, q.v.
s.m. Mountain ebony, Bauhinia variegata (see kaćnār).
s.f. A female of the kaṅćan tribe; a dancing-girl; a harlot, courtezan.
more: n. f. 1. A dancing girl by caste and profession. 2. A woman of the town ( beswā ).
s. f. A female kanchan, a dancing girl, a strumpet, courtezan.

def: s.f. Wish, desire, inclination.
s.m. 'Ear-piercing,' the ceremony of boring a child's ears.
more: n. m. The ceremony of boring a child's ears.

def: s.f. An inferior kind of sweetmeat; — (dialec.) a ceremony after marriage.
more: n. f. A kind of inferior sweetmeat. Damṛī kī kāṅchī, bīvī aur bāṅdī donoṅ nāchi . Boys' rhyme. A farthing worth of sweetmeat get, mistress and slave girl dancing set.
s. (dakh.) A ceremony after marriage: see Qanoon-e-islam, p. 143.

def: s.f. A girdle (esp. one worn by women) furnished with small bells and other ornaments.
s.f. = kāćkūrī, q.v. s.f. The plant Dolichos pruriens; cowhage or cowitch (syn. kawāṅć).
s.f. Slough (of a snake, = keṅćlī, q.v.); — (dialec.) a bodice ( = kaṅćukī).

s.m. = kāć, q.v.; — adj. = kāććā, q.v. s.m. Glass; crystal; an ornament of crystal; quartz; alkaline ashes; any salt of potash or soda in a glassy or crystalline state; a class of diseases of the eye (as gutta serena, &c.)
subs: s.m. Black salt, a medicinal salt (prepared by calcining fossil salt and the fruit of the emblic myrobalan together; it consists chiefly of muriate of soda, with a small quantity of iron, lime, and sulphur, and is a tonic aperient)

def: s.m. (low), A sodomite.
s.m. A bodice, jacket; — skin or slough (of a snake).
s.m. A bodice; a jacket; — armour, mail; — the skin of a snake; short breeches, drawers; clothes.
s.m. (f. -inī), lit. 'Armed, mailed'; an attendant or overseer of the women's apartments, a chamberlain, confidential attendant; — a libidinous man, a debauchee; — a serpent, snake.
s.f. A jacket, a bodice (syn. aṅgiyā).
more: Māṛ. n. f. S. कञ्चुक A bodice ( angiyā ).

def: s.f. A bodice, a vest ( = kanćukī); a sheath, case.
s.f.(m.?) (dialec.), A wall; an embankment.

s.m. Section, part; a single joint (of a reed, or of the stalk or stem of a plant); a slip, stalk; an arrow; section or part (of a book), division, chapter, book; — a bone; — a multitude, heap, quantity; — sport, exhibition.
more: s. m. 1. A section, part, division. 2. Sport, exhibition.
